semantic.role: Infrastructure utility for reading and validating marker-delimited managed blocks in source files and for detecting snapshot output files in the .spine directory. Provides low-level file I/O and git-based file listing capabilities for the writer boundary layer.
responsibilities: Read the content of a managed block (delimited by start/end markers) from a given file path, returning null if the file does not exist.
Check whether a managed block string (or null) contains all required string entries via an every+includes test.
List all snapshot output files tracked by git under a root directory using `git ls-files` with pattern matching.
Check whether the standard snapshot output files (data/ and pages/ subdirectories under .spine) exist on disk.
outOfScope: Writing or modifying managed blocks or snapshot files.
Parsing or validating the content of snapshot files.
Git operations beyond listing tracked files (no commits, diffs, or status checks).
Any external API calls or network I/O.
invariants: managedBlockIncludesAll must receive a block string that was previously read by readManagedFileBlock, or null.
readManagedFileBlock requires that the start and end markers exist exactly once in the file (otherwise returns incorrect slice).
listTrackedSnapshotFiles and snapshotOutputsPresent assume a valid git repository exists at rootDir.
All functions are synchronous and can throw on file system or git errors.

changeIntent.architecturalIntent: Provide reusable, boundary-safe utilities for the writer subsystem to read managed blocks and check snapshot file presence without exposing internal file manipulation logic.
changeIntent.recentChangeIntent: v1.0.0 release; this file establishes the writer boundary infrastructure for governance-driven file management.

publicSurface.description: Interface defining start and end marker strings for a managed block. Used to specify boundaries for readManagedFileBlock.
Reads a file at the given path and extracts the text between the specified start and end markers. Returns null if the file does not exist.
Checks whether a managed block string (or null) contains every entry in the requiredEntries array. Returns false if the block is null.
Executes `git ls-files` in the root directory and returns an array of relative paths matching snapshot file patterns (.json, .md, .svg).
Returns a boolean indicating whether standard snapshot output directories (data and pages) exist under the .spine directory.
